static.description: A minimal Flask REST API that provides a greeting endpoint, a health check endpoint, and a personalised greeting endpoint.
domain_concepts: Greeting: The configurable salutation word (default 'Hello') used in all API responses.
Health Check: A lightweight endpoint (/health) that returns service availability status.

dynamic.architecture_summary: Single-module Flask application exposing three REST endpoints. The app reads configuration from environment variables and serves JSON responses. There is no database or external service dependency; it is a self-contained HTTP server.

key_components.name: app
key_components.responsibility: Defines the Flask application, all route handlers, and the server entry point.

key_components.name: test_app
key_components.responsibility: Contains tests for the Flask application endpoints.

agent_safe_operations: Adding new route handlers in app.py
Adding new test cases in tests/test_app.py
Updating requirements.txt or requirements-dev.txt with new dependencies
Modifying greeting logic or response formats
agent_forbidden_operations: Removing the /health endpoint (used for availability monitoring)
Changing the default host binding (0.0.0.0) without explicit instruction
Removing existing environment variable support (GREETING, PORT)
potential_pitfalls: The app module is a single file (app.py) at the repo root, not a package — imports reference 'app' directly.
The Flask app object and the module share the name 'app', which can cause confusion when importing in tests.
GREETING is read once at module load time; changing the environment variable after import has no effect without reloading.
No requirements-dev.txt content was provided — ensure pytest and pytest-cov are listed there before running tests.
